channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-20
item.description:
Our community has seen significant progress across multiple projects in recent days. Notably, Lakens made key updates to `scienceverse/metacheck`, stabilizing the code_check and enhancing paper database loading functionality. Meanwhile, Gilad Feldman worked on `giladfeldman/docpluck`, adding a new table feature and documenting pre-push canary hook timing, as well as synchronizing `giladfeldman/escicheck` with the latest `effectcheck` version. Other projects, such as `shaheedazaad/auto-zcurve` and `giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward`, also saw updates, including revisions to validation notes and the addition of a Chrome Webstore link, respectively. These connections between projects demonstrate the collaborative spirit of our community, and we celebrate the progress being made across the board. With these updates, our tools are becoming more robust and user-friendly, paving the way for future innovations and discoveries.

119 commits across 7 active repositories this week.

New releases: metacheck v0.1.0-beta

Dormant repos revived: output-it-forward

Most active:

regcheck37 commits
coarse31 commits
docpluck28 commits
metacheck20 commits
escicheck1 commits

item.title: metacheck: 2 commits today
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck

The `fix/code-check-paperlist` branch received a fix to address a code_check crash and resolve the issue of missing paper_id on paper lists, as stated in the commit message "Fix code_check crash and missing paper_id on paper lists". The `papers_databases` branch was updated with new functions to load paper databases from scienceverse/papers, according to the commit "add functions to load paper databases from scienceverse/papers". Both of these changes were made by Lakens on the same day, June 19, 2026. The `fix/code-check-paperlist` branch now has a more stable code_check, and the `papers_databases` branch has enhanced functionality for loading databases. No other branches were worked on in this set of commits. These two branches are the only ones that have been modified since the last update.

2 commit(s) today:

  • 8cbef44 [fix/code-check-paperlist] Fix code_check crash and missing paper_id on paper lists — Lakens
  • b7afa6a [papers_databases] add functions to load paper databases from scienceverse/papers — Lakens
  • 25c15a6 [main] update metacheck page to fix #314 — Lakens
  • 73736b0 [dev] Fix create_report_app() to report_app() in Get Started vignette — Daniel Lakens
  • 00d7953 [ethics_check] add ethics_check + bug fix — Lakens
  • 2b8a367 [new_modules] Revert "fix app instruction #314" — Lakens
  • 1d3719b [new_modules] fix app instruction #314 — Lakens
  • dccb9f4 [main] update module names in vignettes — Lakens
  • 684ccdf [main] Update DESCRIPTION fix #313 — Daniel Lakens
  • 7d7127d [new_modules] fix #313 — Lakens
  • 9749095 [new_modules] explore ideas for z-curve and grim — Lakens
  • a7dea29 [main] Merge pull request #312 from scienceverse/dev — Lisa DeBruine
  • f09fb47 [dev] zenodo citation — Lisa DeBruine
  • 00eaa3b [main] Merge pull request #311 from scienceverse/dev — Lisa DeBruine
  • 767da17 [dev] update apps and docs — Lisa DeBruine
  • 9d012e7 [dev] Merge pull request #310 from nielsvanberkel/main — Lisa DeBruine
  • 8a605a3 [dev] Merge branch 'dev' into main — Lisa DeBruine
  • 3e127fa [dev] Replace devtools::install_github with pak::pkg_install — nielsvanberkel
  • 9ef4d94 [prereg_regcheck] name fix — Lakens
  • 2b0b4e4 [prereg_regcheck] working version of Regcheck — Lakens

View all commits →

item.title: docpluck: 2 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

The `main` branch was worked on, with a focus on documenting the pre-push canary hook timing, as stated in the commit message "document pre-push canary hook timing — never timeout/kill the push". The `main` branch also saw the addition of a feature related to tables, specifically "REQUEST_10 — HTTP-flatten enabling quality fixes + cross-flavor capture" as part of version "v2.4.94". The documentation change and the table feature addition were the two main areas of work in the `main` branch. Only the `main` branch was modified in these commits. The table feature addition included "HTTP-flatten enabling quality fixes" and "cross-flavor capture", as specified in the commit message.

2 commit(s) today:

  • 83ab6bf [main] docs(deploy): document pre-push canary hook timing — never timeout/kill the push — Gilad Feldman
  • 1158b1b [main] feat(tables): REQUEST_10 — HTTP-flatten enabling quality fixes + cross-flavor capture (v2.4.94) — Gilad Feldman
  • 2fd8711 [main] docs(iterate): 2026-06-18 run handoff (v2.4.92 shipped, caption-guard reverted, paused at architectural boundary) — Gilad Feldman
  • c671929 [main] docs(iterate): cycle 2 (v2.4.92 affiliation guard) + cycle 3 (caption-follows REVERTED) journal — Gilad Feldman
  • cf420ca [main] feat(render): never promote affiliation lines to subsection headings (v2.4.92) — Gilad Feldman
  • f392957 [main] docs(iterate): v2.4.91 shipped handoff + cycle journal — Gilad Feldman
  • 7edc62f [main] chore(canary): onboard efendic/maier/xiao into rotating pool (rotation now active) — Gilad Feldman
  • 662c4df [main] @ — Gilad Feldman
  • cfe86f7 [main] feat: add optional extraction guardrails for size and timeout — Gilad Feldman
  • aa82a25 [main] docs(todo): queue investigation for items 3 and 8 — Gilad Feldman
  • 617db41 [main] refactor: surface fallback exception classes in method metadata — Gilad Feldman
  • 24aa793 [main] feat: add fallback telemetry counters and optional logging — Gilad Feldman
  • a5da768 [main] ci: add docs-code consistency guard — Gilad Feldman
  • f8594c3 [main] docs: sync normalization and benchmark claims with current code — Gilad Feldman
  • c8800bd [main] docs: add root README landing page — Gilad Feldman
  • 4c55d6e [main] docs(iterate): 2026-06-16 resume handoff + concurrency-collision lesson — Gilad Feldman
  • 436fc5a [main] refactor(extract_columns): remove orphaned duplicate band re-extractor — Gilad Feldman
  • f7c3729 [main] docs(handoff): RC-1 Step 2 continuation handoff (post-deploy) + canary case-norm todo — Gilad Feldman
  • 818c3d0 [main] docs(iterate): cycle-2 learnings — RC-1 Step 2 + word-preservation-guard-is-the-safety — Gilad Feldman
  • da6c90f [main] docs(iterate): handoff — RC-1 Step 2 first cut shipped (v2.4.90, ship-dark) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: regcheck: 4 commits today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

The `staging` branch was the focus of the recent work, where the `qwen` feature was improved, as stated in the commit message "improve qwen". Additionally, the `qwen` feature was re-added, according to the commit "re-add qwen!". A push was made for redeployment, as indicated by the commit "push for redeploy", likely to apply the changes made. The `staging` branch also saw efforts to resolve an issue, with the commit "hunting down osf bug" suggesting that a bug related to "osf" was being addressed. Furthermore, the logging functionality was enhanced, as described in the commit "improve logging".

4 commit(s) today:

  • f775856 [staging] improve qwen — Jamie Cummins
  • 724d020 [staging] re-add qwen! — Jamie Cummins
  • 89a1470 [staging] push for redeploy — Jamie Cummins
  • baebc81 [staging] hunting down osf bug — Jamie Cummins
  • 2f5eaaa [staging] improve logging — Jamie Cummins
  • 7fb80fe [staging] improve error message — Jamie Cummins
  • a2f7302 [staging] fix osf bug — Jamie Cummins
  • d2089d5 [staging] remove groq endpoints — Jamie Cummins
  • 51b9ba6 [staging] harden json handling — Jamie Cummins
  • 6cdde08 [staging] fix oss bug — Jamie Cummins
  • 1fd2de7 [staging] use more efficient storage — Jamie Cummins
  • 4b7a527 [staging] patch oss SDK handler — Jamie Cummins
  • e514303 [staging] fix oss handling bug — Jamie Cummins
  • 599489b [staging] harden qwen handling — Jamie Cummins
  • 8e7e452 [staging] improve embedding efficiency, purge dead code — Jamie Cummins
  • 88e039c [staging] replace deprecated llama — Jamie Cummins
  • 80dcf34 [staging] harden osf handling again — Jamie Cummins
  • d3471fe [staging] change llama model to best available — Jamie Cummins
  • 65d8ba6 [staging] production hardening - staging test — Jamie Cummins
  • 9c4cd96 [staging] prod preparation: phase 1 + 2 — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →

item.title: output-it-forward: activity update
item.description:
giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward

The `main` branch was updated with a commit that added a Chrome Webstore link for OIF, as stated in the commit message "Add Chrome Webstore link for OIF". No other branches were worked on in this set of commits. This specific change involved adding a link, with no other features or fixes mentioned in the provided commit messages. The commit message directly mentions the addition of the Chrome Webstore link, indicating this was the sole purpose of the commit. There are no other changes or additions noted in the given commits beyond this single update to the `main` branch.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →
